message:
feat(stdio): port BSD funopen over fopencookie (bd-rxgs9)

Adapt BSD's callback-based stdio to glibc fopencookie. funopen has
several signature differences:
  - read/write fns return int (truncated ssize_t), take int byte count
    instead of size_t
  - seek fn takes/returns fpos_t (off_t = i64) directly instead of
    reading/writing through *mut off64_t
  - mode is inferred from which callbacks are NULL (read+write -> "r+",
    read only -> "r", write only -> "w", both NULL -> EINVAL)

Implementation heap-allocates a FunopenTrampoline struct holding the
BSD callbacks plus the user cookie. Adapter functions wrap each call
to translate the size/seek conventions, and the close adapter frees
the trampoline regardless of whether the user supplied a closefn or
the user's closefn returned an error.

Tests: 3 abi tests (EINVAL on no-callbacks, fclose drives user
closefn through the trampoline, full write/seek/read round-trip).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-7thd","title":"syscall: eventfd_write invalid fd should preserve EBADF even for forbidden all-ones payload","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for eventfd_write(-1, UINT64_MAX), so the invalid-fd contract dominates the forbidden payload. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:37:58.438270320Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:10:22.456939420Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:10:22.454702537Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7v4oe","title":"setjmp_abi tests::longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests flakes under high parallelism","description":"## Repro

```bash
for i in {1..8}; do
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test \
    -p frankenlibc-abi --tests -- --test-threads=16 \
    | grep -E "FAILED|invalid size|signal:"
done
```

After ~3-4 iterations the unit test
`setjmp_abi::tests::longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests`
asserts FAILED. Running with `--test-threads=1` does not reproduce.
Found while stress-testing the bd-wbqeo per-thread reentry refactor;
the failure is unrelated to that change (also reproduces against
prior commits — file history shows the test pattern is unchanged).

## Suspected cause

The setjmp/longjmp test uses a shared
`OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, JumpRegistryEntry>>>` keyed by env
pointer address (crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/setjmp_abi.rs:44). The
unit test creates a stack-local `marker: [u64; 16]` and uses its
address as the env_addr key. Two test functions running on different
worker threads get distinct stack addresses, but a single worker
thread that runs tests sequentially reuses the same stack frame
addresses across tests — the registry entry from a prior test
remains for that key and is overwritten by the next capture. The
new entry's bytes should match the new capture's mem_bytes, but
under contention or specific scheduling the read-back of mem_bytes
in restore_env compares against a stale or partially-updated entry,
returning EFAULT (22) instead of triggering the deferred
ENOSYS-panic path the test asserts.

The test then panics with errno=22 reason=`invalid_or_foreign_jump_context`
instead of errno=38 reason=`deferred_longjmp_transfer_backend`.

## Fix candidates

1. Test-only: each test should remove its registry entry on exit
   (RAII guard that calls `registry().lock().remove(&env_addr)`
   when the test scope ends).
2. Production: registry could key on (thread_id, env_addr) instead
   of just env_addr — this would handle both intentional cross-
   thread longjmp (illegal per POSIX, EINVAL is correct) and the
   stack-frame-reuse case across sequential tests on the same worker.
3. Test-only: serialize the setjmp tests with a #[serial] attribute
   or a shared TEST_LOCK Mutex so they don't race for stack address
   reuse.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3) — test-only flake, doesn't affect production behavior.
But it does mask real failures by polluting CI noise. Suggest fix #1
(simplest) or #3 (most robust) as the swarm has time.","notes":"Fixed in 6ee22896: RegistryEntryGuard RAII wrapper added to all six setjmp_abi unit tests. Validated by 12x stress with --test-threads=16: zero longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests failures. Three OTHER unrelated flakes surfaced in same sweep — filed separately.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:26:42.985457536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:35:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:35:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7xkx","title":"resolv: characterize safe divergence for getaddrinfo_a NULL list with positive nitems","description":"Host glibc segfaults for getaddrinfo_a with a NULL request list and positive nitems. FrankenLibC currently takes a safe fallback instead of crashing. Capture that crash boundary and document the intended safety divergence so future resolver work does not accidentally copy host UB.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:24.046955751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:29:21.388130149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:29:21.385958008Z","close_reason":"Characterized the host getaddrinfo_a NULL-list crash boundary in a subprocess and documented FrankenLibC's intentional safe divergence for positive nitems with a NULL request list.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-83q3a","title":"PORT: fgetwln (NetBSD libutil wide-char line reader)","description":"NetBSD libutil fgetwln(stream, *lenp): wide-char counterpart of fgetln. Read a line of wchar_t from stream up to and including the trailing newline, return pointer into a thread-local wchar_t buffer plus length (in wchars) via *lenp. Returns NULL on EOF/error with *lenp=0. Buffer invalidated by next call. Mirror existing fgetln impl over fgetc; here loop over fgetwc and accumulate Vec<libc::wchar_t> in a thread_local RefCell.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:20:36.993395628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:20:36.993395628Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-83q3a","title":"PORT: fgetwln (NetBSD libutil wide-char line reader)","description":"NetBSD libutil fgetwln(stream, *lenp): wide-char counterpart of fgetln. Read a line of wchar_t from stream up to and including the trailing newline, return pointer into a thread-local wchar_t buffer plus length (in wchars) via *lenp. Returns NULL on EOF/error with *lenp=0. Buffer invalidated by next call. Mirror existing fgetln impl over fgetc; here loop over fgetwc and accumulate Vec<libc::wchar_t> in a thread_local RefCell.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:20:36.993395628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:24:03.412748139Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T14:24:03.410666417Z","close_reason":"shipped: fgetwln in wchar_abi.rs over fgetwc + 3 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-rqn","title":"Production kernel manifest: minimal required math set + compile-time gates","description":"Critique mapping: #5.

Deliverables:
- Define default production kernel (small, explainable, benchmarked).
- Add compile-time feature gates separating production vs research modules.

Acceptance:
- Default build excludes ResearchOnly modules.
- Production kernel meets strict/hardened latency budgets.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"Perf gate rerun on idle host passed (scripts/perf_gate.sh): strict deltas within threshold, hardened decide_observe delta +1.74%. However target budget checks still rely on allow_target_violation policy in perf gate, and manifest currently keeps all runtime_math modules in production with research_only_modules empty. some runs failed open to local due worker sync instability (no-space/file-vanished on remote rsync).","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:37:03Z"},{"id":1139,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Additional bd-rqn progress (BronzePine): updated scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to support manifest partition semantics.\n\nDetails:\n- Manifest extraction now normalizes both  and .\n- Admission checks run over union(manifest sets), while ADMITTED status is restricted to modules in .\n- Production-core completeness now checks specifically against production set.\n- Admission ledger now emits  and  booleans in addition to .\n\nValidation:\n- python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py => status=pass, errors=0\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test => PASS (3/3)","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:41:34Z"},{"id":1140,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"BronzePine","text":"Correction for prior note formatting: updated scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to support manifest partition semantics. Validation: python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py -> pass; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test -> PASS (3/3).","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:41:49Z"},{"id":1141,"issue_id":"bd-rqn","author":"AzureBluff","text":"Progress update (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): completed production-kernel partition and governance artifact alignment to unblock bd-3ot.1.

Implemented:
- tests/runtime_math/production_kernel_manifest.v1.json: moved to minimal production set (25 modules) and explicit research_only_modules (44 modules).
- tests/conformance/math_production_set_policy.v1.json: updated change_gate.manifest_sha256 to b47cd1f6283dc3bb151fb0c57b7f8ef5c1b1afe526cf2b333d92eecc37ef257b, module_count=25, and summary counts to production-only.
- tests/conformance/math_retirement_policy.json: reflected completed migration state (RC-1=0, no active waivers, migration waves=0, summary updated).
- tests/runtime_math/runtime_math_classification_matrix.v1.json: synchronized per-module in_production_manifest / in_research_only_manifest flags and summary counts with new manifest partition.
- Re-ran scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to regenerate:
  - tests/runtime_math/admission_gate_report.v1.json
  - tests/runtime_math/controller_manifest.v1.json

Gate/script verification (PASS):
- scripts/check_runtime_math_manifest.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_classification_matrix.sh
- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh
- scripts/check_math_retirement.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_admission.sh
- scripts/check_math_governance.sh

RCH validation:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_classification_matrix_test (PASS 8/8)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_production_set_policy_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_retirement_test (PASS 9/9)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets started remotely and progressed through broad suite but stalled in long pthread lifecycle tests; process was terminated to avoid indefinite hang.

This resolves bd-rqn’s outstanding blocker note: production manifest is now minimal, research-only routing is explicit, and policy artifacts are auditable + reproducible.
","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-rth1","title":"bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads","description":"Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z","close_reason":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"],"comments":[{"id":1142,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"},{"id":1143,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

Implemented:
- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in  (tombstone semantics preserve lookup chains).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback () to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI integration tests in :
  - 
  - 
  - shared guard + native-threading force helper for deterministic setup.

(B[m[32m+    let total_size = GUARD_PAGE_SIZE
(B[m[32m+        .checked_add(stack_size)
(B[m[32m+        .ok_or(crate::errno::ENOMEM)?;

     // Allocate the full region as read+write.
     // SAFETY: anonymous mmap with no fd, valid parameters. ✅
-  ✅

+{"id":"bd-rxgs9","title":"PORT: funopen (BSD callback-based stdio over fopencookie)","description":"BSD funopen(cookie, readfn, writefn, seekfn, closefn): callback-based custom stdio stream, the BSD analog of glibc fopencookie. Different conventions: read/write fns return int (truncated ssize_t), take int byte count; seek fn takes/returns fpos_t (off_t = i64) directly instead of via *offset pointer; mode is inferred from which callbacks are NULL (readfn+writefn=r+, readfn only=r, writefn only=w; both NULL=EINVAL). Implement by allocating a heap trampoline holding the BSD callbacks + original cookie, building a CookieIoFuncs that adapts each call (clamp size to INT_MAX, route seek through *offset), passing the trampoline pointer as the fopencookie cookie, and freeing the trampoline in the close trampoline regardless of whether user closefn succeeded.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T14:28:23.613184776Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T14:28:23.613184776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ryp2g","title":"_IO_adjust_column / _IO_adjust_wcolumn diverge from glibc — wrong column-counter algorithm","description":"## Defect

Both `_IO_adjust_column` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs:2055`)
and `_IO_adjust_wcolumn` (line 2091) implement their own tab-aware
column tracker:

```rust
for i in 0..count as usize {
    let byte = ...;
    match byte {
        b'\n' | b'\r' => c = 0,
        b'\t' => c = (c + 8) & !7,
        _ => c += 1,
    }
}
```

The actual glibc implementation (sunrpc-adjacent — `libio/genops.c`) is:

```c
int _IO_adjust_column (unsigned start, const char *line, int count) {
    const char *ptr = line + count;
    while (ptr > line)
        if (*--ptr == '\n')
            return line + count - ptr - 1;
    return start + count;
}
```

The semantics is: **find the position past the last newline in the
buffer, OR add `count` to `start` if no newline.** Tabs are NOT
expanded; carriage returns are NOT zeroed; the function never iterates
forward through every byte.

This is an ABI behavioral divergence. Any consumer (e.g. older glibc-
linked C++ stream code, certain pretty-printers in third-party
libraries) that calls `_IO_adjust_column` to know "how far am I from
the last newline" gets a different answer from us than from real
glibc, especially when the buffer contains tabs or carriage returns.

Additional smaller defects in the current code:
- `c + 8` and `c += 1` arithmetic on `c: u32` panics in debug mode
  for adversarial column values near `u32::MAX` (theoretical only —
  caller would need to seed `col` near INT_MAX which is implausible).
- `c as c_int` at the end can return a negative result when `c >
  i32::MAX`.

## Fix

Rewrite both helpers to mirror glibc's "scan back from end for last
newline" algorithm. Keep the membrane policy gate intact. The wide
variant scans backward over `i32` wide-chars looking for `0x0A`.

## Severity

[HIGH] (P2). Pure correctness divergence — no memory-safety
exploit, but breaks ABI compatibility for third-party libraries that
depend on the documented glibc semantics. Pretty-printer column
counters, indent trackers, and any consumer that mixes
_IO_adjust_column results with raw text-position arithmetic will
silently produce wrong layouts.","notes":"Fixed in fb6e5ed2: _IO_adjust_column and _IO_adjust_wcolumn rewritten to match glibc libio/genops.c — backward scan for last newline, returns either (count - newline_pos - 1) or (start + count). Tabs and CR no longer special-cased. Saturating arithmetic eliminates the debug-mode overflow panics in the old tab-expansion path. 6 regression tests pin glibc semantics. Verified 52/52 io_internal_native_file_test pass + clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:09:59.243184856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:15:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:15:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-rzk00","title":"PORT: __getppid + __gettid + __getuid/euid/gid/egid + __setuid/gid/euid/egid + __setsid + __setpgrp (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the 12 glibc reserved-namespace aliases for process-id and credentials entry points: __getppid, __gettid, __getuid, __geteuid, __getgid, __getegid, __setuid, __setgid, __seteuid, __setegid, __setsid, __setpgrp. glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the symbols some statically-linked NSS modules + libpthread internals link against. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers delegating to the public counterparts in unistd_abi (process_abi for setpgrp on some glibcs). Tests verify each alias agrees with its public counterpart on the current process state; mutator aliases (setuid/setgid/etc) are tested only for their EPERM-on-non-root error path so they dont break the test environment.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:15:03.639884955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:19:30.087613347Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:19:30.085440627Z","close_reason":"shipped: 12 glibc reserved-namespace pid/cred aliases (__getppid + __gettid + __getuid/euid/gid/egid + __setuid/gid/euid/egid + __setsid + __setpgrp) + 6 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
